agent.system_template:
You are controlling a robot in a real indoor environment (a photorealistic 3D scan of a building). You interact only through these tools:

- observe(): look through the robot's forward-facing camera (RGB image plus a clearance readout: meters to the nearest obstacle in the left/center/right thirds of the view; 10.0 = open).
- step(actions): execute movement actions in order. 0 = STOP (permanently ends the episode — declares you have reached the goal), 1 = move forward 0.25 m, 2 = turn left 15 degrees, 3 = turn right 15 degrees.
- look_around(): one call returning four labeled views (ahead / right / behind / left); rotates 360 degrees and restores your heading (costs 24 turn steps).

Your task is to follow this navigation instruction to its endpoint:

"{{ task }}"

Rules:
- Alternate observing and stepping: look, decide where the instruction wants you to go next, move, look again.
- You have a budget of {{ step_budget }} movement actions; each step() result reports roughly how many remain.
- You succeed only if you issue action 0 (STOP) while within 3 meters of the instruction's endpoint. STOP is permanent — issue it only when you believe you are at the goal.
- Turning in place (e.g. step([2,2,2,2,2,2])) is a cheap way to look around when unsure.
- Work autonomously until you stop; nobody can answer questions.
agent.instance_template: Begin navigating. Call observe() first to see where you are.

tools.name: observe
tools.description:
Look through the robot's forward-facing camera.

Returns the current egocentric RGB view plus a clearance readout: distance in meters to the nearest obstacle in the left/center/right thirds of the view (10.0 = open, 10 m or more). If the goal object is centered ahead, clearance "center" is your true distance to it. Pure read — does not advance the simulator or consume step budget.

tools.name: look_around
tools.description:
Scan the surroundings in ONE call: rotates a full 360 degrees and
    returns four labeled views — ahead (0°), right (+90°), behind (+180°),
    left (+270°) — then restores the original heading exactly.

    Costs 24 low-level turn steps from the step budget but only one tool
    call. Use this at junctions and decision points instead of turning 15
    degrees at a time; your position and final heading are unchanged.

tools.name: step
tools.description:
Execute a sequence of movement actions, in order.

Actions: 0 = STOP (permanently ENDS the episode — issue it only when you believe the robot is within 3 meters of the goal), 1 = move forward 0.25 m, 2 = turn left 15 degrees, 3 = turn right 15 degrees.

Executes sequentially and halts early if the episode ends. Returns how many actions ran, total steps taken, remaining budget, and whether the episode is over. The camera view changes after stepping — call observe() to see the result. Note: when plenty of budget remains, your FIRST STOP request is withheld pending a placement check — call step([0]) again to confirm and execute it.

messages.content:
You are controlling a robot in a real indoor environment (a photorealistic 3D scan of a building). You interact only through these tools:

- observe(): look through the robot's forward-facing camera (RGB image plus a clearance readout: meters to the nearest obstacle in the left/center/right thirds of the view; 10.0 = open).
- step(actions): execute movement actions in order. 0 = STOP (permanently ends the episode — declares you have reached the goal), 1 = move forward 0.25 m, 2 = turn left 15 degrees, 3 = turn right 15 degrees.
- look_around(): one call returning four labeled views (ahead / right / behind / left); rotates 360 degrees and restores your heading (costs 24 turn steps).

Your task is to follow this navigation instruction to its endpoint:

"Walk across the floor and wait the archway. "

Rules:
- Alternate observing and stepping: look, decide where the instruction wants you to go next, move, look again.
- You have a budget of 500 movement actions; each step() result reports roughly how many remain.
- You succeed only if you issue action 0 (STOP) while within 3 meters of the instruction's endpoint. STOP is permanent — issue it only when you believe you are at the goal.
- Turning in place (e.g. step([2,2,2,2,2,2])) is a cheap way to look around when unsure.
- Work autonomously until you stop; nobody can answer questions.
Begin navigating. Call observe() first to see where you are.
I'll start by observing my surroundings.

message.content: I'll start by observing my surroundings.

messages.content: I see a room with a pool table. I need to find an archway. Let me scan around to locate it.

message.content: I see a room with a pool table. I need to find an archway. Let me scan around to locate it.

messages.content:
I can see archways in multiple directions. The behind view (+180°) shows clear archways leading to another room. The right view also shows arched openings. The instruction says "walk across the floor and wait [at] the archway."

The behind view shows the most prominent archways (dark wood arched doorways). Let me turn around to face that direction and move toward the archway.

message.content:
I can see archways in multiple directions. The behind view (+180°) shows clear archways leading to another room. The right view also shows arched openings. The instruction says "walk across the floor and wait [at] the archway."

The behind view shows the most prominent archways (dark wood arched doorways). Let me turn around to face that direction and move toward the archway.

messages.content:
Now I'm facing the room with archways. I can see two archways ahead - one in the center-left leading to another room, and one on the right. The chairs and sofa are in front of me. Let me move toward the archways, navigating around the furniture. The center clearance is 3.9m.

Let me move forward carefully. There's a sofa/chair blocking the direct center. Let me aim slightly right toward the open archway path.

message.content:
Now I'm facing the room with archways. I can see two archways ahead - one in the center-left leading to another room, and one on the right. The chairs and sofa are in front of me. Let me move toward the archways, navigating around the furniture. The center clearance is 3.9m.

Let me move forward carefully. There's a sofa/chair blocking the direct center. Let me aim slightly right toward the open archway path.

messages.content: Now I can clearly see an archway ahead in the center - a dark wood arched doorway leading to another space. The path leads directly toward it. Let me continue forward toward the archway.

message.content: Now I can clearly see an archway ahead in the center - a dark wood arched doorway leading to another space. The path leads directly toward it. Let me continue forward toward the archway.

messages.content: The archway is clearly visible ahead in the center - a dark arched doorway leading into a hallway. I'm getting closer. Let me continue forward toward it. It appears slightly left of center now.

message.content: The archway is clearly visible ahead in the center - a dark arched doorway leading into a hallway. I'm getting closer. Let me continue forward toward it. It appears slightly left of center now.

messages.content: I'm now very close to the archway - it's clearly visible ahead in the center, a beautiful arched doorway. The center clearance is 3.3m. Let me move a bit closer to be right at the archway.

message.content: I'm now very close to the archway - it's clearly visible ahead in the center, a beautiful arched doorway. The center clearance is 3.3m. Let me move a bit closer to be right at the archway.

messages.content:
I'm now right at the archway - the arched doorway is directly ahead of me, and I can see through it into a hallway beyond. The center clearance is 2.6m, meaning I'm at the threshold of the archway. This is exactly where the instruction wants me to "wait [at] the archway."

I've walked across the floor and arrived at the archway. This is the endpoint. Let me issue STOP.

message.content:
I'm now right at the archway - the arched doorway is directly ahead of me, and I can see through it into a hallway beyond. The center clearance is 2.6m, meaning I'm at the threshold of the archway. This is exactly where the instruction wants me to "wait [at] the archway."

I've walked across the floor and arrived at the archway. This is the endpoint. Let me issue STOP.

content.text: {"stop_withheld": true, "message": "STOP not executed (first request). You still have 87 tool calls \u2014 verify placement before committing: (1) look_around(); (2) re-read the instruction's FINAL clause word by word; (3) apply the placement rules (several similar spots -> the one farther along your travel direction; 'all the way / to the end' -> keep going until center clearance < 1.5 m; 'door/doorway' -> stand at the opening). Move if a better spot exists, then call step([0]) again to execute it.", "tool_calls_used": 13, "tool_calls_remaining": 87}

info.message: STOP not executed (first request). You still have 87 tool calls — verify placement before committing: (1) look_around(); (2) re-read the instruction's FINAL clause word by word; (3) apply the placement rules (several similar spots -> the one farther along your travel direction; 'all the way / to the end' -> keep going until center clearance < 1.5 m; 'door/doorway' -> stand at the opening). Move if a better spot exists, then call step([0]) again to execute it.

The system is asking me to verify placement. The instruction says "wait [at] the archway." I'm right at the archway opening now. Let me confirm my position is good — I'm at the arched doorway threshold. This is exactly where I should "wait at the archway."

Let me verify by looking around to make sure this is the best archway position.

The instruction says to "walk across the floor and wait [at] the archway" — I've crossed the floor from the pool table room and am now standing at the archway threshold. This is the endpoint.

I'll confirm STOP.
